nodes.purpose: Reduces an FFT power spectrum into 10 normalized 0..1 frequency buckets (hz32…hz16k) with attack/decay smoothing.
nodes.useWhen: You need '10 frequency buckets as 0.0–1.0' (or similar named band levels) from a spectrum. Feed the hz… outputs into your visualizer or any reactive node.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need the full dense spectrum rather than a handful of bands (use audio-fft's magnitudes directly).

nodes.purpose: FFT of a PCM sample window into a power spectrum (floatArray of 1024 magnitude bins). Pure DSP — no audio device.
nodes.useWhen: You have a PCM sample window (e.g. from microphone.macos) and need its frequency spectrum. Feed `magnitudes` into audio-bands.
nodes.avoidWhen: You only need a few named band levels — pair this with audio-bands rather than computing the FFT inline.

nodes.purpose: Blends two textures with a selectable Photoshop-style mode (add/multiply/screen/overlay/soft light/hard light/difference), mixed by opacity.
nodes.useWhen: You need to composite/mix two texture layers. Takes TWO texture inputs — base and blend — plus a mode enum and an opacity slider. The go-to node for combining a generator (gradient/noise/checkerboard) over a camera or other source.
nodes.avoidWhen: You only have one texture to process (use a single-input tone/color node like brightness), or you need alpha-aware over compositing with premultiplied coverage — this mixes RGB by a scalar opacity and preserves base alpha.

nodes.purpose: Scale RGB by a gain (exposure) — the simplest tone adjustment.
nodes.useWhen: You need to brighten or darken an image by a linear gain, or a minimal reference for a single-pixel color op.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need nonlinear midtone control (use gamma) or contrast around a pivot (use contrast).

nodes.purpose: Provides the built-in/selected Mac camera feed as an MTLTexture.
nodes.useWhen: You need live camera input as a texture source.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a still image or a non-camera video source.

nodes.purpose: Generates a two-color checkerboard pattern as a texture source (no input).
nodes.useWhen: You need a tiled checkerboard — UV/alignment test pattern, transparency backdrop, or a starting point for any parity-tiled procedural generator. Tune cell density with scale and the two colors.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need an organic or irregular pattern (use noise), a smooth ramp (use gradient), or want to modify an existing texture (this ignores input).

nodes.purpose: Radially splits the red and blue channels from a center point to fake lens color fringing.
nodes.useWhen: You want lens-distortion fringing, a glitchy RGB split, or edge color separation.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need physically accurate per-wavelength dispersion or barrel distortion.

nodes.purpose: Expand or compress RGB contrast around an adjustable pivot.
nodes.useWhen: You need to boost or flatten contrast after brightness, with control over the fixed midpoint.
nodes.avoidWhen: You want a nonlinear midtone curve (use gamma) or a per-channel color grade.

nodes.purpose: 3x3 Sobel on luminance; edges take edgeColor, flat regions take bgColor.
nodes.useWhen: You want outlines, a toon/blueprint look, or an edge mask from any image.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need sub-pixel edges, Canny-quality thinning, or true variable line width.

nodes.purpose: Apply a 1/gamma power curve to lift or crush midtones.
nodes.useWhen: You need nonlinear midtone brightening/darkening without clipping highlights or shadows.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a linear gain (use brightness) or true sRGB/linear color-space conversion.

nodes.purpose: Separable 9-tap Gaussian blur (two 1D render passes with a lazy intermediate texture).
nodes.useWhen: You want to soften an image, prep a bloom/glow, or reduce high-frequency detail; radius 0–20.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a very heavy blur (chain instances instead) or an exact box/median filter.

nodes.purpose: Generate a two-color gradient (linear, radial, or angular) as a texture source.
nodes.useWhen: You need a smooth color ramp — background, mask, or the base layer of a composite. Pick type and two colors; no input.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a hard-edged pattern (use checkerboard) or organic noise (use noise), or want to modify an existing texture.

nodes.purpose: Adds animated per-pixel hash noise to fake film/sensor grain; churns over time via speed.
nodes.useWhen: You want moving film grain or sensor noise over a live image (reads ctx.time).
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a static dither or deterministic frame-locked noise (this drifts with time).

nodes.purpose: Load a still image file as a texture source, scaled to the viewport.
nodes.useWhen: You need a fixed image as input to an effect chain — a permission-free, camera-free source. Set the path and a fit mode.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a moving image (use video-file) or a live camera feed (use camera.macos).

nodes.purpose: Live microphone capture as a 2048-sample mono PCM window (floatArray), with a synthetic-audio fallback when no mic is authorized.
nodes.useWhen: You need live audio as a stream of PCM samples to analyze (the source of an audio-reactive pipeline). Feed `samples` into audio-fft.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a texture/visual source, or an audio file rather than the live mic.

nodes.purpose: Generates animated procedural noise (white, value, fractal fBm, or voronoi) as a grayscale texture source.
nodes.useWhen: You need procedural grayscale noise for masks, displacement, dissolves, or organic motion. Pick a type (white/value/fractal/voronoi), set scale for detail, and drive animation with speed (reads ctx.time); octaves controls fractal richness.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need color output, a deterministic still pattern (use checkerboard), or precise SDF/geometry — this is stochastic grayscale only.

nodes.purpose: Quantizes the image into square blocks by snapping each pixel to its block's top-left texel.
nodes.useWhen: You want a blocky mosaic / censor / retro-downsample look; block size 1–64 px.
nodes.avoidWhen: You want smooth downscaling or antialiased blocks — this snaps to hard texels by design.

nodes.purpose: Blend RGB toward luma to desaturate, restore, or oversaturate.
nodes.useWhen: You need a saturation control, or a grayscale/desaturate pass (set amount 0).
nodes.avoidWhen: You need hue rotation or selective per-color saturation.

nodes.purpose: Binarize the image by luma into a soft-edged black/white mask.
nodes.useWhen: You need a luma matte or key source with a feathered cutoff.
nodes.avoidWhen: You want to preserve original color, or need chroma/alpha keying.

nodes.purpose: Play a local video file as a live texture source, scaled to the viewport.
nodes.useWhen: You need a video clip as input to an effect chain — a permission-free alternative to the camera. Set path, loop, rate, and fit.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a still image (use image-file) or a live camera feed (use camera.macos).

nodes.purpose: Fade the frame edges toward a color with a soft radial falloff.
nodes.useWhen: You want classic edge darkening or a colored edge tint framing the subject.
nodes.avoidWhen: You need a centered spotlight/mask output rather than an edge blend, or a non-radial shape.
